Capital Markets: Sentiment Stable, Even Somewhat Amused. Currently, the stock and cryptocurrency markets have not experienced significant volatility; the S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average rose only slightly (approximately 0.5%) after the news was released. Analysts believe the market had already anticipated this outcome. Investors believe that although the tariffs were rejected, due to Trump's other executive actions and the lengthy tax refund process, the cost pressure on businesses will not disappear immediately in the short term. This "positive" news has already been priced in.

3. Subsequent Impact: What Will Happen Next?

Although the tariffs were rejected, the chain reaction they triggered has only just begun.

A trillion-dollar "tax wave": Walmart, Amazon, and tens of thousands of small and medium-sized importers have already begun consulting lawyers.

It is estimated that the total amount of tariffs collected since 2025 will exceed $175 billion. If this money is to be refunded, it will be a huge hole for the U.S. Treasury.

It is estimated that the total amount of tariffs collected since 2025 will exceed $175 billion. If this money is to be refunded, it will be a huge hole for the U.S. Treasury.

...

Easing Inflationary Pressures: Economists predict that if these tariffs are completely lifted, the average American family's annual living expenses could decrease by approximately $1,500, which would be beneficial in alleviating current inflation.

Trade Game Enters "Deep Waters": Since the "swift, accurate, and ruthless" executive orders are no longer effective, the Trump administration may adopt more targeted and covert trade restrictions in the future.

For the global supply chain, although uncertainty has decreased, the main theme of the "game" remains unchanged.

data.title: Trump announces signing of executive order imposing 10% tariffs on global imports.
data.description: On February 20, local time, US President Trump posted on his social media platform "Real Social" that he had just signed a bill in the Oval Office imposing a 10% global import tariff on all countries, which would take effect immediately. The US Supreme Court issued a ruling that morning, finding that the International Emergency Economic Powers Act did not authorize the president to impose large-scale tariffs, representing a major setback for the Trump administration's tariff policy. In response, Trump claimed there were many "other options," stating that the US government would also launch several investigations under Section 301 of the Trade Act of 1974, allegedly for "unfair trade practices," "to protect our country from unfair trade practices by other countries and companies." Trump also posted on social media that day, stating that all tariffs imposed by the United States on the grounds of "national security," as well as tariffs imposed under Section 232 of the Trade Expansion Act of 1962 and Section 301 of the Trade Act of 1974, will remain in effect. Additionally, Trump acknowledged at a press conference that the Supreme Court ruling will trigger a protracted legal battle, potentially "lasting five years," regarding whether the federal government must refund billions of dollars in tariffs to American businesses.

data.title: Golden Morning News | SBF Denies Multiple Allegations; Netherlands Bans Polymarkets
data.description:
Headline

▌SBF Releases Lengthy Article Denying Multiple Allegations Surrounding Him and FTX

FTX founder SBF released a lengthy article titled "10 Misconceptions About Me and FTX," addressing ten accusations against him and FTX, including denying claims that "FTX is insolvent, has evaporated $8 billion in customer assets," "partnered in multiple relationships," and "was forced into bankruptcy due to inability to raise funds." He claimed to have received offers to fill liquidity gaps, but the bankruptcy filing was handled by lawyers. Furthermore, he clarified controversies surrounding margin trading liquidity, whether bankruptcy constitutes a crime, and the flow of political donations.

▌Dutch regulator bans Polymarket from operating in the country

The Dutch regulatory body (Ksa) has fined Polymarket operator Adventure One QSS Inc. for illegally operating gambling activities. The Ksa has ordered Polymarket to cease operations in the country or face a fine of €420,000 ($462,000) per week, with a maximum fine of €840,000 (approximately US$924,000).

... **Market Updates** As of press time, according to CoinGecko data: BTC price is $67,287.49, up 1.1% in the last 24 hours; ETH price is $1,959.38, down 0.5% in the last 24 hours; BNB price is $608.14, up 0.5% in the last 24 hours; SOL price is $82.99, up 1.7% in the last 24 hours; DOGE price is $0.09892, up 0.3% in the last 24 hours. XRP price is $1.42, down 0.1% in the last 24 hours; TRX price is $0.2848, up 2.1% in the last 24 hours; WLFI price is $0.1168, down 1.9% in the last 24 hours; HYPE price is $29.46, up 3.5% in the last 24 hours. **Policy** **Trump:** He has signed an order imposing a 10% global tariff on all countries, and the 10% tariff will take effect almost immediately, according to Jinse Finance.

▌Trump: Supreme Court Did Not Overturn Tariffs, Will Impose 10% Global Tariffs on Existing Tariffs

US President Trump: The Supreme Court did not overturn the tariffs, it only denied the use of IEEPA. A 10% global tariff will be imposed on top of existing tariffs. All existing national security tariffs will take full effect immediately. He will not seek further authorization from Congress on tariffs, and "I don't need it."

▌Trump Plans to Invoke New Trade Authorization, May Impose New Comprehensive Tariffs on Trading Partners

According to the New York Times, Trump plans to invoke new trade authorization following the Supreme Court's ruling. It is understood that Trump is considering imposing new comprehensive tariffs on trading partners. Section 301 tariffs are one of the tools under discussion. The White House declined to comment. Some officials expect Trump to announce the new authorization at a press conference on Friday afternoon local time, but they warned that the plan is subject to change.

▌Strategist: Market Should Not Have Overly High Expectations for Supreme Court Tariff Ruling

Ebury market strategist Matthew Ryan said that the market should not place too high expectations on the US Supreme Court's decision to overturn Trump's global trade tariffs. He stated that this decision was widely expected, and regardless, Trump is likely to quickly move to other methods to implement trade restrictions.

... Ryan said, "(Trump) has multiple means at his disposal to circumvent this ruling. This means that while we may see some short-term disruptions, his long-term tariff strategy is unlikely to go off track as long as the White House can replicate this system through alternative methods." BNP Paribas Asset Management recently announced the issuance of a tokenized share of a French domestic money market fund on the Ethereum blockchain through its AssetFoundry™ platform. The project employs a "permissioned access model," ensuring that only qualified and authorized participants can hold and transfer assets, in compliance with relevant regulatory requirements. This is BNP Paribas' second tokenized money market fund issuance, designed to test the complete end-to-end process from issuance and transfer agency to tokenization and public blockchain connectivity.

▌Dubai Launches Secondary Market for Real Estate-Backed Tokens

The Dubai Land Department (DLD) and tokenization company Ctrl Alt announced on Friday the launch of a secondary market for real estate-backed tokens, allowing the resale of partial property ownership worth $5 million. The tokens, based on XRP Ledger, are backed by title deeds, trading is synchronized with the Dubai Land Registry, and powered by Ripple Custody. This project is part of Dubai's plan to tokenize $16 billion worth of real estate by 2033.

▌Kraken Integrates with ICE Chat, Expanding Institutional OTC Crypto Trading Channels

Kraken announced a new integration with ICE Chat, the instant messaging platform owned by the Intercontinental Exchange (ICE), enabling institutional traders to connect directly with Kraken's over-the-counter (OTC) trading division through this widely used communication network.

This move integrates Kraken's OTC execution capabilities into the institution's existing workflows, lowering the entry barrier and improving access to cryptocurrency liquidity in the spot and options markets. Three researchers from the Federal Reserve System published a paper on February 12th, arguing that data from the prediction market platform Kalshi can reflect macroeconomic expectations more in real time and should be included in the Fed's decision-making framework. The authors include Federal Reserve Chief Economist Anthony Diercks, Research Assistant Jared Dean Katz, and Johns Hopkins University researcher Jonathan Wright. The study compared Kalshi data with traditional surveys and market-implied expectations, concluding that Kalshi can provide high-frequency, continuously updated probability distributions to measure the risk-neutral probability of macroeconomic indicators such as interest rate decisions. The researchers noted that Kalshi reacts more quickly after major policy statements or economic data releases. However, this Fed research paper is for internal discussion and does not represent a formal policy stance.

▌Uniswap has launched a proposal to increase protocol fees

Uniswap announced on X that a proposal to increase protocol fees has been launched. This update aims to enable fees for all remaining v3 liquidity pools on the mainnet and for v2 and v3 on the eight newly added chains.

Cryptocurrency

▌A Bitcoin OG deposits another 6,318 Bitcoins into Binance

According to Onchain Lens data, a Bitcoin OG has deposited another 6,318 Bitcoins into Binance, worth $424.86 million.

To date, it has deposited a total of 11,318 bitcoins into Binance, worth $760.61 million.

▌Coinbase Includes Robo Token in Listing Roadmap

According to an official announcement from Coinbase, Coinbase Markets stated that it has added Robo Token (ROBO) to its asset listing roadmap.

▌“BTC OG Insider Whale” Transfers Another 5,000 BTC to Binance

According to Lookonchain monitoring, the “BTC OG Insider Whale” has transferred another 5,000 BTC to Binance, worth approximately $335.75 million. Analysis: Bitcoin's outlook depends on market sentiment; fair value estimated at around $75,000. UniCredit strategist Thomas Strobel stated that Bitcoin's recent decline primarily reflects weak market sentiment and macroeconomic pressures, even with easing concerns about US regulation and reduced policy risks. The bank currently maintains a neutral stance, estimating Bitcoin's fair value at around $75,000. A price drop of approximately 35% from that level, especially a sustained stay below $50,000, could indicate a deeper structural shift. A rebound would likely require improved market sentiment, increased ETF inflows, and a more favorable liquidity environment.

▌Coinbase Institutional Address Transfers 767 BTC to Unknown Wallet

According to WhaleAlert monitoring, Coinbase Institutional transferred 767 BTC, worth approximately $51.66 million, to an unknown wallet.

▌Ripple Adds $20 Million to RLUSD Supply, Totaling 1.53 Billion

Ripple has minted an additional $20 million worth of RLUSD, bringing the total supply to 1.53 billion, targeting the regulated stablecoin market currently dominated by USDT ($183 billion) and USDC ($74 billion).

**Important Economic Developments** **▌US Economic Growth Expected to Reach 2.2% in 2025, Lowest Level Since 2021** **On February 20th local time, the US Bureau of Economic Analysis reported that the US real gross domestic product (GDP) is projected to grow by 2.2% year-on-year in 2025, lower than the 2.8% growth in 2024, marking the lowest level since 2021.** In terms of prices, the gross domestic price index is expected to rise by 2.6% in 2025, higher than the 2.4% in 2024. The personal consumption expenditures (PCE) price index is expected to rise by 2.6% for the year, unchanged from 2024. The core PCE price index, excluding food and energy prices, is expected to rise by 2.8%, slightly lower than the 2.9% in 2024. Preliminary estimates indicate that US GDP will grow at an annualized rate of 1.4% in the fourth quarter of 2025 (October to December). Compared to 4.4% in the third quarter, economic growth slowed significantly in the fourth quarter. The report points out that fourth-quarter GDP growth was mainly driven by increased consumer spending and investment, but this was partially offset by declining government spending and exports.

▌Federal Reserve's Bostic: I think the neutral interest rate could be 0.25 to 0.5 percentage points lower than the current policy rate

Federal Reserve's Bostic: I think the neutral interest rate could be 0.25 to 0.5 percentage points lower than the current policy rate. If inflation "goes astray" and starts to rise, the Fed will have to put the option of raising interest rates on the table.

▌Traders bring forward their expectations for a Fed rate cut to June

Traders have brought forward their expectations for a Fed rate cut to June.

    ▌The US Dollar Index (DXY) fell 20 points in the short term, reaching a low of 97.71

    The US Dollar Index (DXY) fell 20 points in the short term, reaching a low of 97.71.

    Golden Encyclopedia

    ▌What are Long and Short Positions?

    Long and short positions represent opposing strategies used by investors and traders to anticipate price movements of an asset under consideration. In the cryptocurrency space, going long and short still apply to concepts from traditional financial markets. To profit from a rise in cryptocurrency prices, going long means buying it and expecting its value to increase over time. In contrast, going short in the cryptocurrency market means selling a cryptocurrency that you don't actually own in anticipation of a price drop, then buying it back at a cheaper cost to close the position and profit from the price decline. Cryptocurrency traders and investors utilize these strategies to navigate the high volatility and speculative nature of digital assets and to capitalize on opportunities in both bullish and bearish market conditions.

    In cryptocurrency trading, a long position begins by buying an asset in the hope that its price will rise, while a short position begins by disposing of an asset (usually borrowed) in the hope that its price will fall. Closing a position means buying the asset at a lower price to gain profit, while exiting a long position means selling the asset at a higher price to lock in profits. Entry and exit points are crucial for the successful implementation of these strategies. Cryptocurrency long positions have the potential to generate substantial profits through price appreciation, but they also come with significant risks of market volatility and potential losses. Despite these risks, cryptocurrency long positions have the potential to generate significant returns. The opportunity to profit from price increases is the primary benefit. Short cryptocurrency positions can be rewarding by betting on price declines, but they also carry significant risks due to market volatility, unlimited potential losses, and unpredictable price increases. Shorting cryptocurrencies requires precise timing, meticulous risk management, and continuous market monitoring to successfully navigate inherent volatility and maximize potential gains while limiting losses.
